task_completion.description: Open the barcode type settings in bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application is open and the settings/preferences dialog specifically showing barcode types or symbologies is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Code 128 barcode type is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the barcode settings menu, the checkbox or toggle next to 'Code 128' is checked or set to the 'enabled' state.

task_completion.description: Ensure the PDF417 barcode type is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the barcode settings menu, the checkbox or toggle next to 'PDF417' is checked or set to the 'enabled' state.
In the barcode settings menu, checkboxes next to other 1D barcodes such as EAN-13, EAN-8, UPC-A, UPC-E, Code 39, Codabar, and ITF/Interleaved 2 of 5 are unchecked or set to the 'disabled' state.
In the barcode settings menu, checkboxes next to other 2D barcodes such as QR Code, DataMatrix, and Aztec are unchecked or set to the 'disabled' state.

task_completion.description: Save or apply the configured barcode settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks an 'OK', 'Apply', or 'Save' button in the settings dialog, or the settings window is closed after the correct selections are made, returning to the main bcWebCam interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard bcWebCam application windows and dialogs, not a text editor or terminal window modifying configuration files directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely navigated the application to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps like opening menus, clicking checkboxes, and standard OS window focus states, rather than just displaying a static image of the final state.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the task requirements into a notepad or search bar.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Code 128' and 'PDF417' are seen within the application's native settings UI, not typed out in a separate text editor or command prompt.
